title: WBJEE 2026 Notification Out: Registration (March 10), Dates, Link, Eligibility

detail.title: WBJEE 2026 Notification Out: Registration (March 10), Dates, Link, Eligibility
detail.short_info: WBJEE 2026 notification released March 5. Registration open now (March 10) at wbjeeb.nic.in. Exam on May 24, eligibility, step-by-step apply guide, fees, dates & direct link. Secure Engineering/Pharmacy seats in West Bengal.

sections.heading: WBJEE 2026 Notification Out: Registration (March 10), Dates, Link, Eligibility

content.text: WBJEE 2026 notification released March 5. Registration opens today, March 10, at wbjeeb.nic.in.

rows: West Bengal Joint Entrance Examinations Board (WBJEEB) ​
Paper 1: Mathematics (75 questions, 100 marks); Paper 2: Physics & Chemistry (40 each, 100 marks total) ​
Admission to UG Engineering, Technology, Pharmacy, Architecture in West Bengal colleges ​

content.text: This exam opens doors to top West Bengal engineering colleges for Class 12 graduates nationwide. Check the official brochure for complete rules.
Registration is live now-apply early to secure your spot. Late fees may apply after initial deadlines.
Candidates need to pass Class 12 with Physics, Mathematics, and Chemistry/Biology. Minimum 45% aggregate marks (40% for SC/ST/OBC). Born on or after January 1, 2008 (18+ by December 31, 2026).
Domicile isn't required for general seats, but reserved categories get state benefits. JEE Main scores may supplement in some cases.​
Upper age relaxations apply for SC/ST/OBC/PwD. English must be a Class 12 subject.

sections.heading: How to Download WBJEE 2026 Notification PDF

content.text: This document has all syllabus, fees, and reservation details. Print a copy for your records.
Use only the official portal linked above. Online-only process with no postal applications.​

items: Enter name, DOB, email, mobile, and set password.
Verify with OTP sent to email/mobile.
Pay fee and submit; download receipt.

content.text: Double-check entries-errors lead to rejection. Save login credentials.​
₹500 (General/OBC males); ₹400 (Females/SC/ST/3rd gender). Non-refundable, pay via net banking/UPI/card.​

Paper 1 (Math): 2 hours, 100 marks. Paper 2 (Physics/Chemistry): 2 hours, 100 marks. MCQs in three categories with partial/negative marking. Syllabus: Class 11-12 topics from Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics.
Focus on high-weightage chapters like Calculus, Mechanics. Total 200 marks determine rank.​
Avoid unofficial sites to prevent scams. Check for updates regularly.
